name: BaaS Misconfiguration Patterns
description: Patterns for detecting insecure Backend-as-a-Service configurations (Supabase, Firebase)

patterns.name: Supabase RLS Policy USING (true)
patterns.description: RLS policy allows access to all rows. Any user (including anonymous) can access every row in this table.
patterns.remediation: Replace USING (true) with a condition that checks auth.uid(), e.g., USING (auth.uid() = user_id).

patterns.name: Supabase RLS WITH CHECK (true)
patterns.description: RLS write policy allows any data to be written. No row-level write restrictions.
patterns.remediation: Replace WITH CHECK (true) with a condition that validates ownership, e.g., WITH CHECK (auth.uid() = user_id).

patterns.name: Supabase Anon Write Access
patterns.description: Write access granted to anonymous/public role. Unauthenticated users can modify data.
patterns.remediation: Change TO anon to TO authenticated and add auth.uid() checks in USING/WITH CHECK.

patterns.name: Supabase Service Role in Frontend
patterns.description: Supabase service_role key exposed via frontend environment variable. This key bypasses all RLS policies.
patterns.remediation: Remove the public prefix. Use this key only in server-side code (API routes, serverless functions).

patterns.name: Supabase SECURITY DEFINER with Anon Access
patterns.description: Database function with SECURITY DEFINER is callable by anonymous users. The function runs with the definer's privileges, bypassing RLS.
patterns.remediation: Change GRANT to 'authenticated' role and add auth.uid() checks inside the function.

patterns.name: Supabase Admin in Client Code
patterns.description: Supabase admin auth methods used in potentially client-accessible code. Admin methods require service_role key.
patterns.remediation: Move admin operations to server-side API routes or edge functions.

patterns.name: Firebase Allow All Read/Write
patterns.description: Firebase security rules allow unrestricted read or write access. Any user can access/modify data.
patterns.remediation: Replace 'if true' with auth checks: 'if request.auth != null && request.auth.uid == userId'.

patterns.name: Firebase Realtime DB Open Rules
patterns.description: Firebase Realtime Database rules allow unrestricted read access.

patterns.name: Firebase Realtime DB Open Write Rules
patterns.description: Firebase Realtime Database rules allow unrestricted write access.

patterns.name: Firebase Permissive Wildcard Rule
patterns.description: Firebase wildcard rule matches all documents with permissive access. This overrides more specific rules.
patterns.remediation: Remove the wildcard catch-all or set it to 'allow read, write: if false'. Define specific rules per collection.

patterns.name: Firebase Write Without Data Validation
patterns.description: Firebase write rule only checks authentication, not data shape or content. Any authenticated user can write arbitrary data.
patterns.remediation: Add data validation: check request.resource.data.keys(), field types, and size limits.

patterns.name: Firebase Storage Without Size/Type Limits
patterns.description: Firebase Storage write rule does not enforce file size or content type limits.

patterns.name: Firebase Test Mode Rules (Expiring)
patterns.description: Firebase rules using test-mode timestamp. These are meant to be temporary and should be replaced with proper rules before deployment.
patterns.remediation: Replace timestamp-based rules with proper auth-based security rules.

patterns.name: BaaS Public Storage Bucket
patterns.description: Storage bucket configured as public. All objects are accessible without authentication.
patterns.remediation: Make bucket private unless public access is intentionally required (e.g., static assets). Use signed URLs for private files.
